title: hydrocotyle burmanica subsp. handelii
intro: Hydrocotyle burmanica subsp. handelii, also known as Indian pennywort, is a plant species belonging to the hydrocotyle genus, commonly found in the tropical regions of Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Indian pennywort include improved cognitive function, reduced anxiety and stress, and enhanced liver function, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies for various health issues. Indian pennywort is often used in herbal preparations such as teas, capsules, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional Ayurvedic medicine to promote overall well-being and longevity.
